name: knowledge_graph_query
description: Traverse the knowledge graph to find relationships between named entities. Use this skill for questions like: 'Who does [analyst] cite?', 'Which sources are connected to [topic]?', 'What is the relationship between [person A] and [person B]?'. Returns entities and typed relation paths. Entities are populated from ingested KB content via the entity extractor. If the graph is sparse (few ingested items), results may be limited.

entity.description: Entity name or account ID to start traversal from. Accepts full name, canonical name, alias, or account_id from kb_accounts. Fuzzy substring matching is applied if no exact match found.

relation_type.description: Filter to a specific relation type. Options: 'mentioned', 'cited', 'endorsed', 'criticized', 'replied', 'writes_about', 'appeared_on', 'co_authored', 'contradicts', 'predicts', 'works_for'. Null returns all relation types.

max_hops.description: Maximum BFS traversal depth. 1 = direct relations only. 2 = friends-of-friends (default). 3 = three degrees of separation. Higher values return more entities but increase latency.

user_id.description: User context. Currently unused for graph filtering (graph is global). Pass from session context for future user-scoped graphs.

root_entity.description: The resolved starting entity. Null if not found.

entities.description: All entities within max_hops of the root entity. Each has: id, name, canonical_name, entity_type, aliases, mention_count, first_seen, last_seen.

relations.description: All traversed relation edges. Each has: id, source_entity, source_name, target_entity, target_name, relation_type, weight, evidence_count, first_seen, last_seen.

resolved_from.description: Non-null when fuzzy resolution was used: e.g. '"Fed"→"Federal Reserve"'. Mention in your answer so the user knows which entity was queried.

error.description: Non-null if the root entity was not found. Inform the user and suggest they check the entity name or note that the graph may not contain this entity yet.
